name: Yorkshire Pudding
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat the eggs until foamy.
2. Add the milk and beat again.
3. Gradually add the flour, beating constantly until the mixture is smooth.
4. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
5. Refrigerate the batter for one hour while the beef is roasting.
6. When the roast is done, remove it to a carving board and pour off all juices and all but three tablespoons of fat (reserve the excess fat and juices for gravy).
7. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
8. Return the roasting pan with the fat to the oven and heat until the fat sizzles.
9. Pour the cold batter into the pan and bake at 400 degrees Fahrenheit for 15 minutes.
10. Lower the temperature to 350 degrees Fahrenheit and continue baking for another 15 minutes.
11. The pudding should be puffy and golden when cooked through. Serve immediately with roast beef and gravy.
